arts.ttl: Merson says: If Maresca doesn't win the next two he'll be sacked by Chelsea
arts.sum: Sky Sports' Paul Merson says Chelsea boss Enzo Maresca needs his team to beat both Southampton and Leicester to save his job after a recent underwhelming run.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid transfer news: Spanish giants to rival Manchester United, Chelsea for France international?
arts.sum: Real Madrid are reportedly contemplating whether to join the race for RB Leipzig defender Castello Lukeba, who is also attracting interest from Premier League teams.

arts.ttl: Rudderless Chelsea’s sustained slump risks making Cole Palmer restless
arts.sum: The 22-year-old is an elite player. How might he react if the Blues miss out on Champions League qualification again? Remember those chants of “We’ve got our Chelsea back” when Enzo Maresca’s side thumped Southampton at St Mary’s in December? Life moves quickly in west London. Two months have passed since those heady days, back when the talk was of a burgeoning title challenge, and the mood at Stamford Bridge has fractured so rapidly that the theme of a protest against the owners before their home game against Southampton on Tuesday night is – wait for it – “We want our Chelsea back”. It may seem an overreaction to a dip in form. Even so it is not hard to find fans who feel a growing disconnection with the club and struggle to understand Chelsea’s youth-driven recruitment strategy. They sense that the change pushed by the majority shareholder, Clearlake Capital, has been too intense; that too much knowledge has left the building since the end of the Roman Abramovich era. Perhaps it is why Chelsea, who have built a squad full of raw talent but short of experienced leaders, look rudderless when games go against them. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Cole Palmer struggling – so who is Chelsea’s player of the season so far?
arts.sum: It seems like just yesterday that we were watching Enzo Maresca's first games as Chelsea manager, wondering what a new coach would bring in terms of style and changes to last season. Well the season has flown by, and we're now 2/3rds of the way through the campaign already. It's time to start looking ahead […]

arts.ttl: Report: ‘Brilliant’ Chelsea player is ‘very close’ to injury return, he could give Cole Palmer a much-needed break

arts.ttl: Chelsea transfer news: Blues send scouts to watch £40m Enzo Maresca favourite?
arts.sum: Chelsea are reportedly stepping up their pursuit of Ipswich Town striker Liam Delap, sending scouts to watch the 22-year-old at Portman Road.
